seo_title: Meet EAGLE 3.1: The Speculative Decoding Algorithm That Fixes…
meta_description: EAGLE 3.1 fixes speculative decoding instability in production, addressing attention drift in LLM inference.
body_html:
EAGLE 3.1 fixes attention drift in LLM inference by addressing speculative decoding instability in production.

Improves accuracy and reliability in LLM inference Reduces computational overhead and memory usage Enhances robustness against adversarial attacks

Stability Issue Speculative Decoding Instability

Comparison with Existing Algorithms Comparing EAGLE 3.1 to existing algorithms, such as the Earley parser, reveals its improved performance in addressing attention drift in LLM inference.

Future Work and Research Directions

Improving the robustness of EAGLE 3.1 against adversarial attacks Exploring its applications in real-world scenarios Developing a comprehensive proposal for advancing XAI

source.title: Meet EAGLE 3.1: The Speculative Decoding Algorithm That Fixes Attention Drift in LLM Inference
source.summary: Researchers have released EAGLE 3.1, a speculative decoding algorithm that aims to fix attention drift issues in large language model (LLM) inference. The update is the result of collaboration between the EAGLE team, vLLM, and TorchSpec. It addresses instability in production environments caused by speculative decoding.
